title: Directions Quiz
objectives: Understand the fundamentals of Directions Quiz.

questions.question: North means:
South means:
East means:
West means:
Right turn is:
Left turn is:
Directions are based on:
Best method:
Final position depends on:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Direction + distance.

questions.question: Directions are used in:
A walks 5 km north, then 3 km east. What is A's final direction from start?
questions.explanation: Net displacement has north and east components → North-East.

questions.question: B walks 4 km west, then 4 km south. Which direction from the start?
If C moves 2 km east and returns 2 km west, where is C now?
questions.explanation: Equal opposite moves cancel; C returns to start.

questions.question: D faces north and makes a right turn then another right turn. Which direction now?
E walks 3 km south, 3 km east, 3 km north. What is E's displacement direction from start?
questions.explanation: South and north cancel → net 3 km east.

questions.question: If a person goes north then makes two left turns, final direction is:
A faces east and turns anticlockwise 90°. Which way is he facing?
If someone goes 10 km north then 10 km east, the shortest path back to start is in which direction?
questions.explanation: Return vector is opposite of northeast, i.e., southwest.

questions.question: P moves 7 km east and Q moves 7 km north from same point. Direction from P to Q is:
questions.explanation: From P (east of start) to Q (north of start) vector is northwest.

questions.question: A is 5 km north of B. B is 3 km west of C. Direction of A from C is:
questions.explanation: A is north of B, B west of C → A is NW of C.

questions.question: If X walks 2 km north, 2 km west, 2 km south, final position is:
questions.explanation: North and south cancel; net 2 km west.

questions.question: If a man faces south and turns 270° clockwise, which direction does he face?
A starts at origin and goes 3 east, 4 north. What is direction from start to final point?
questions.explanation: Both east and north displacement positive → North-East.

questions.question: If somebody walks 1 km north and returns to starting point, which is true?
options: He must have walked in a loop
questions.explanation: To return, he must walk south 1 km back to start.

questions.question: A walks 6 km east then 8 km north. Distance from start is:
If person turns right from west-facing position, which direction next?
If two people move equal distances in perpendicular directions, the direction from one to the other is:
questions.explanation: Perpendicular displacements produce a diagonal relative direction.

questions.question: A is due north of B. C is due east of B. Which direction is C from A?
questions.explanation: From A (north of B) to C (east of B) → go south then east → South-East.

questions.question: If someone faces north and makes three consecutive left turns, final direction is:
questions.explanation: Three left turns = 270° left = 90° right → North→East.

questions.question: If a person moves 5 km south and then 12 km east, the bearing of the final point from the start is approximately:
questions.explanation: Angle = arctan(5/12) ≈ 22°; direction is east then 22° south → E 22° S.
